description_long: This is a bulbous ceramic vase with a wide, rounded body and a narrow, slightly flared neck, featuring a pronounced spout and two small, stubby side handles. The surface is adorned with intricate cobalt blue floral and foliate motifs on a cream-colored background, reminiscent of traditional Majolica or Delftware pottery. The blue patterns are dense and symmetrical, with a central medallion on the front and leafy scrollwork wrapping around the body. The rim and spout show some irregularities and chipping, suggesting age or wear. The interior appears unglazed and dark, contrasting with the ornate exterior. This vase would be a striking decorative piece in a public or semi-public urban setting, such as a plaza, park, or entryway.
description: A rounded ceramic vase with blue floral patterns and a pronounced spout, featuring small side handles and visible surface wear.
description_view_0: Front view shows the ornate blue floral motif with a central medallion and the wide spout opening.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the spout's irregular edge and one of the small side handles, with dense blue patterning.
description_view_2: The back view displays a more simplified blue motif and the rounded body, with less emphasis on the spout.
description_view_3: This side view reveals both stubby handles and the full curvature of the vase, with the blue foliate design wrapping around.
